summary:
Geniculate neuralgia occurs due to issues with the facial nerve (Cranial Nerve VII) as this area includes the geniculate ganglion which can be affected by neuralgia leading to pain symptoms typically associated with damage or irritation here. This condition primarily impacts sensory fibers involved in taste sensation from part of the tongue along with salivary gland function among other functions controlled via CNVII. 

The summary has been condensed into two focused statements highlighting critical aspects related directly back towards addressing what causes such a form of neuralgia based upon anatomical location described initially through terms like “geniculate”.
